title: Valid RTLIL produces invalid Verilog, which Yosys then cannot read back

features.name: reduction operator on zero-width signal
features.description: Code should include a reduction OR (or reduce_bool) operation applied to a zero-width signal, which logically evaluates to 0 but may produce invalid Verilog output such as |{ } with an empty concatenation

features.name: empty concatenation list in expression
features.description: Code should include an empty concatenation {} used as an operand, which is syntactically illegal in Verilog (IEEE 1364-2005 A.8.1) and can be produced by tools when emitting zero-width signal expressions

features.name: cell with zero-width input parameter
features.description: Code should include a cell (e.g. reduce_bool) where the A_WIDTH parameter is set to 0 and the input connection is empty, representing a zero-width signal fed into an operator
features.snippet:
cell $reduce_bool $2
  parameter \A_SIGNED 0
  parameter \A_WIDTH 0
  parameter \Y_WIDTH 1
  connect \A {}
  connect \Y $1
end
